title: cyrtophyllum caudatum
intro: Cyrtophyllum caudatum, also known as the fish bone fern, is a species of fern native to tropical Asia. This plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Cyrtophyllum caudatum include reducing swelling, pain, and inflammation, as well as promoting wound healing and improving respiratory health. The plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and it is also used in traditional herbal medicine in the form of a paste or powder, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
